metadata.curriculum: ACARA v9 (Australian Curriculum, Assessment and Reporting Authority)

skills.name: Homophones
skills.description: Words that sound the same but have different meanings

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Understand how to identify and use homophones
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students can identify homophones in context and use them correctly in writing

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Recognise and use homophones and homophones in sentences
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students demonstrate understanding through proofreading and editing

contexts.description: Friend posted: 'I through the ball to.' Identify spelling error.
Email: 'Their going to the beach.' Which homophone is wrong?
Story uses 'wear' instead of 'where'. Identify and fix.

skills.name: Apostrophes (Contractions & Possessives)
skills.description: Using apostrophes in contractions (don't) and possessives (cat's)

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Use apostrophes in contractions and possessives
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students accurately use apostrophes in simple contractions and possessives

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Distinguish between different types of apostrophes
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students distinguish between contractions and possessives and use correctly

naplan_alignment.item_format: Multiple choice - select correct apostrophe use

contexts.description: 'Don't worry,' she said. Which is correct: dont, don't, don't?
Correct the sentence: 'The cats toys are in the box.'

skills.name: Compound Words
skills.description: Words made from two smaller words

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Identify compound words
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students recognize and can split compound words into component parts

naplan_alignment.item_format: Drag and drop - match halves to make compound words

contexts.description: Recipe says 'Add butter to the cupcake mixture.' Identify compound word.

skills.name: Synonyms & Antonyms
skills.description: Words with similar meanings (happy/joyful) and opposite meanings

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Understand synonyms and antonyms
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students can identify and use synonyms and antonyms in context

naplan_alignment.item_format: Multiple choice - select synonym or antonym

contexts.description: In the story, 'bright' is used. Which synonym would fit: shiny, dark, large?
Ad says 'This product is STRONG!' What's an antonym?

skills.name: Reading Comprehension
skills.description: Understanding written passages and answering questions

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Read and understand texts to retrieve information
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students demonstrate understanding of literal and inferred information

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Analyse how texts use language to communicate ideas
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students analyze author's purpose and infer implied information

naplan_alignment.item_format: Multiple choice - answer after reading passage

contexts.description: Read short narrative. Answer: What was the character's problem?
Read article about climate. Infer: Why was this information important?
Read news article. Answer: What does the headline suggest will happen?

skills.name: Verb Tenses (Past, Present, Future)
skills.description: Understanding and using correct verb tenses

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Use past, present and future tenses mostly appropriately
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students use correct tenses in writing and speaking with few errors

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Use past, present and future tenses appropriately
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students consistently use correct tenses across all writing

naplan_alignment.item_format: Fill-in-blank or multiple choice - select correct tense

contexts.description: Complete: 'Yesterday, I _____ (go) to the park.'
Fix tense: 'She go to school every day.' (was/is/will be)

skills.name: Place Value (Units, Tens, Hundreds)
skills.description: Understanding place value in whole numbers

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Understand place value: units, tens, hundreds
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students can identify place value and represent numbers in multiple ways

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Recognise and represent three-digit numbers
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students partition and represent three-digit numbers in expanded form

naplan_alignment.item_format: Multiple choice - identify place value of digit

contexts.description: If price is $347, the 4 is in the _____ place.
In the year 2025, what place value does the 2 represent?

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Recognise and describe one-half, one-quarter, three-quarters, one-third
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students identify fractions of shapes and can partition shapes into equal parts

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Recognise, find and write fractions
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students represent fractions and recognize equivalent fractions

naplan_alignment.item_format: Multiple choice or visual - identify fraction of shape

contexts.description: Recipe needs 3/4 cup flour. You have 1/4. How much more?
Pizza cut into 4 pieces. You eat 1. What fraction remains?
Shirt on sale for 1/2 off original $40 price. Sale price?

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Add and subtract two-digit numbers
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students add and subtract two-digit numbers using flexible strategies

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Add and subtract three-digit numbers
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students use efficient strategies to add and subtract three-digit numbers

naplan_alignment.item_format: Multiple choice or fill-in - solve addition/subtraction

contexts.description: You have $50. Buy item for $17. How much left?
Team A scored 45 points. Team B scored 38. Difference?

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Represent and solve multiplication problems
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students represent multiplication as repeated groups and arrays

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Recall multiplication facts
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students recall multiplication facts for 2x, 3x, 5x, 10x tables

naplan_alignment.item_format: Multiple choice - solve fact or word problem

contexts.description: Each apple costs $3. You buy 5. Total cost?
20 marbles shared equally among 5 friends. Each gets?

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Represent odd and even numbers
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students identify odd and even numbers and understand the pattern

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Investigate and describe patterns
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students extend patterns and describe the rule

naplan_alignment.item_format: Multiple choice or fill-in - continue pattern

contexts.description: Pattern: 2, 4, 6, 8, _____. What's next?
Calendar pattern: 1, 8, 15, 22, _____. (every 7 days)

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Compare and order objects by length
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students compare and order objects by length using direct comparison

contexts.description: If one block is 5cm, and you stack 4, total height?

acara_standards.content_descriptor: Identify and describe story elements
acara_standards.achievement_standard: Students identify character, setting, and major events in narratives

naplan_alignment.item_format: Multiple choice or short answer - identify story element

contexts.description: In story, character was brave because... (identify motivation)
